description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a single-edged metal blade. The blade is painted red on its upper portion, with a sharp, exposed cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The handle shows a natural wood grain with some wear, suggesting frequent use, and is ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip. The blade is securely mounted to the handle using a classic wedged fitting, with no visible screws or bolts. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative engravings or branding visible. This type of axe is commonly found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or used by municipal workers for chopping wood or clearing debris.
description: A traditional hand axe with a red-painted metal blade and a curved wooden handle, showing signs of use and a secure wedged mounting.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, clearly showing the red blade, wooden handle, and the overall curved profile.
description_view_1: A top-down view highlights the length and curvature of the handle, as well as the thickness of the blade.
description_view_2: A front-on view of the blade edge, emphasizing the sharpness and width of the cutting surface.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the full length of the handle and the flat poll of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and a red-painted upper section; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
